fact: In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many squirrels realize they forgot where they buried half their nuts and start panicking.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities on December 3, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
December 3 falls during Sagittarius season, when people born under this sign are supposedly extra adventurous and might randomly decide to climb a tree.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, December 3 is when summer is just getting started and kids are probably begging to go swimming.
Many evergreen trees are pumping extra sap on dates like this to protect themselves from freezing, kind of like how you put on extra socks when it's cold.
December 3 is only 18 days away from the winter solstice in the north, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2 minutes every single day.
Polar bears in the Arctic are in their happiest season right now because the sea ice is finally thick enough for serious seal hunting.
December 3 is the 337th day of the year, and 337 is a prime number that can't be divided by anything except 1 and itself.
In cold regions, December 3 is when your breath becomes visible clouds, which happens when the air temperature drops below about 45 degrees Fahrenheit.
Many deciduous trees have been completely naked for weeks by December 3, standing around like giant wooden skeletons until spring.
December 3 is positioned perfectly in early winter when snow might stick to the ground for the first time in northern climates, creating that magical crunchy sound under your boots.
